issue.weather: Cloudy w/ chance of patch notes

ticker.text: Pragmata launches to 97% positive on Steam — Capcom delivers
GTA 6 confirmed for November 19 — Rockstar enters release fiscal year
Hades II now on PS5 and Xbox — day one on Game Pass
Saros goes gold — Housemarque's Returnal follow-up locks April 30 date
Epic Games reportedly building a Disney extraction shooter separate from Fortnite

lead.title: Capcom's Moon Game Was Worth Every Delay
lead.dek: Six years of cryptic trailers, broken release windows, and meme-worthy silence — and then Pragmata landed and reminded everyone why patience in gaming still means something. A deep look at the year's first must-play.
lead.author: Romello Morris

poll.question: Which game from this week are you picking up first?

calendar.title: Saros
calendar.sub: PS5 Exclusive · Deluxe Early Access Apr 28

spotlight.title: The Cartoon Detective Shooter Nobody Saw Coming
spotlight.blurb: Mouse: P.I. For Hire renders an entire city in 1930s rubber-hose animation and wraps a boomer shooter inside it. It's a debut that has no right being this confident — and at $39.99, it's the easiest recommendation of the week.

comments.name: Marcus T.
comments.body: Pragmata 9.2 is not a surprise to anyone who played the demo. That hacking mechanic alone sold me — 2 million demo downloads don't lie.

comments.name: Jade W.
comments.body: Hades II on PS5 at 120fps is genuinely something else. Console players were sleeping on what PC had — glad the wait is finally over.

comments.name: Chris B.
comments.body: Pokemon Champions is rough right now but I'm not writing it off yet. Among Us took 2 years to find its audience. Give it a few patches.

comments.name: Aisha M.
comments.body: Mouse P.I. might be my sleeper pick of 2026. Nobody was talking about it before launch and now it's everywhere. Classic indie moment.
